audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with extensive ancestral Scripture memory; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for trust definition, ancestors, women, suffering, better resurrection, cloud of witnesses setup.

verses.restored: Now trust is substance/assurance of things hoped for, evidence/conviction of matters not seen.
For by this the elders were witnessed.
By trust we understand the ages to have been framed by word of Divine, so that what is seen has not come from things appearing.

By trust Abel offered to the Divine a better sacrifice than Cain, through which he was witnessed to be righteous/just, the Divine witnessing upon his gifts; and through it, having died, he still speaks.
By trust Enoch was transferred so as not to see death, and he was not found because the Divine transferred him; for before the transfer he had been witnessed to have pleased the Divine.
But apart from trust it is impossible to please, for the one drawing near to Divine must trust that he is and that he becomes rewarder of those seeking him.

By trust Noah, having been warned concerning things not yet seen, moved with reverent care, prepared ark for salvation/healing of his household, through which he condemned the world-system and became heir of righteousness/justice according to trust.
By trust Abraham obeyed when called to go out to place he was about to receive as inheritance, and he went out not knowing where he was going.
By trust he sojourned in land of promise as foreign land, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, fellow heirs of same promise.
For he awaited the city having foundations, whose artisan and maker is Divine.
By trust Sarah herself received power for seed/offspring, even beyond season of age, since she considered trustworthy the One promising.
Therefore also from one, and these things having been made dead, were born as stars of heaven in multitude and like innumerable sand by seashore.
According to trust all these died, not having received the promises, but seeing them from afar and greeting them, and confessing that they were strangers and sojourners upon earth.
For those saying such things make clear that they seek homeland.
And if indeed they remembered that from which they went out, they would have had opportunity to return.
But now they desire better, that is, heavenly; therefore the Divine is not ashamed of them, to be called their Divine, for he prepared for them a city.
By trust Abraham, being tested, offered Isaac; and the one receiving promises was offering the only/beloved,
to whom it was spoken: In Isaac your seed will be called.
reckoning that Divine was able even from dead ones to raise, from which also, in parable, he received him back.
By trust Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things coming.
By trust Jacob, dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph and worshiped upon top of his staff.
By trust Joseph, ending life, remembered concerning the exodus of the sons of Israel and gave command concerning his bones.
By trust Moses, having been born, was hidden three months by his parents, because they saw the child was beautiful, and they did not fear the decree of the king.
By trust Moses, having become great, refused to be called son of Pharaoh's daughter,
choosing rather to suffer hardship with the people of the Divine than to have temporary enjoyment of sin/misalignment,
considering reproach of Messiah greater wealth than treasures of Egypt, for he was looking away to the reward.
By trust he left Egypt, not fearing anger of the king, for he endured as seeing the unseen One.
By trust he has kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, so that the destroyer of firstborn might not touch them.
By trust they passed through Red Sea as through dry land, which the Egyptians, attempting, were swallowed.
By trust walls of Jericho fell, having been encircled for seven days.
By trust Rahab the sex worker did not perish with those disobeying, having received the scouts with peace.
And what more shall I say? For time will fail me telling of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David and Samuel and the prophets,
who through trust conquered kingdoms, worked righteousness/justice, obtained promises, shut mouths of lions,
quenched power of fire, escaped mouths of sword, were empowered from weakness, became strong in war, turned back camps of foreigners.
Women received their dead by resurrection; others were tortured, not accepting release, so that they might obtain better resurrection.
Others received trial of mockings and whippings, and further, chains and imprisonment.
They were stoned, sawn in two, tested, killed by sword; they wandered in sheepskins, goatskins, lacking, pressured, mistreated,
of whom the world-system was not worthy, wandering in deserts and mountains and caves and holes of the earth.
And all these, having been witnessed through trust, did not receive the promise,
the Divine having foreseen something better concerning us, so that apart from us they might not be completed.

translation_notes: Trust is pistis: assurance/faithfulness/trust embodied across ancestors.
Women are active bearers of trust: Sarah, Moses' parents, Rahab, women receiving their dead.
The chapter honors Israel's ancestors; do not appropriate them in ways that erase Jewish identity.
safety_note: The suffering list honors endurance but should not romanticize persecution or imply people must remain in abuse. Hebrews 11 is witness, not coercion.
